title: Termin AppSpec IR
description: The complete Intermediate Representation of a compiled Termin application. Produced by the Termin compiler, consumed by any conforming Termin runtime.

ir_version.description: Semantic version of the IR schema. Runtimes should reject versions they don't support.

reflection_enabled.description: Whether the runtime should expose Reflection endpoints (/__reflect/*) for operational visibility.

app_id.description: Globally unique application identifier (UUID). Generated by the compiler on first compile and written back to the source file. Used by deployment systems to match packages to existing deployments for upgrades vs new installs.

name.description: Human-readable application name from the DSL 'Application:' declaration.

description.description: Application description from the DSL 'Description:' declaration.

auth.description: Authentication and authorization configuration. Null only for the simplest apps (anonymous access).

content.description: All Content definitions — the data model. Each becomes a persistent storage table.

access_grants.description: Resolved access control rules: which scopes grant which verbs on which Content.

state_machines.description: State machines governing lifecycle transitions on Content (or Channels/Computes/Boundaries).

events.description: Event rules: triggers (CRUD or CEL conditions) that cause side-effects.

routes.description: Resolved HTTP API routes. Generated by the compiler from Content + AccessGrants.

pages.description: Presentation layer: component trees defining the UI for each user story.

nav_items.description: Navigation menu items with role-based visibility.

streams.description: Server-Sent Event stream definitions for real-time updates.

computes.description: Compute modules: pure data transformations (Transform, Reduce, Expand, Correlate, Route).

channels.description: Channel definitions: declared data flow paths with direction and delivery guarantees.

boundaries.description: Boundary definitions: trust and isolation zones grouping Content and other primitives.

error_handlers.description: Error handling rules with retry, escalation, and notification actions.

reclassification_points.description: Declared points where data changes confidentiality level through a Compute module. The compiler extracts these from Compute definitions that have output_confidentiality_scope set. Runtimes use this list for audit logging and policy enforcement.

QualifiedName.description: A resolved identifier with three forms. The compiler generates all three from the DSL display name. Runtime implementers should use 'snake' for storage/API keys and 'display' for UI rendering.

snake.description: Snake_case form for storage tables, API paths, and internal keys: 'stock_levels'.

pascal.description: PascalCase form for class names and type references: 'StockLevels'.

FieldType.description: Storage-level column type. Maps to SQL column types in relational backends. TEXT is the default. JSON is used for list-typed fields.

FieldSpec.description: A single field in a Content schema. Combines the DSL's semantic business_type with a resolved storage column_type.

name.description: Snake_case field name, used as the storage column name and API key.

display_name.description: Original human-readable field name from the DSL: 'unit cost', 'parent order'.

business_type.description: Semantic type from the DSL. Runtimes use this for input validation, formatting, and UI hints. 'reference' means a foreign key to another Content. 'enum' means a constrained set of values. 'automatic' means a system-managed timestamp (created_at, updated_at). 'datetime' is a user-entered date+time.

column_type.description: Resolved storage type. The compiler maps business_type to column_type (e.g., currency -> REAL, whole_number -> INTEGER).

required.description: Whether the field must have a non-null value on create.

unique.description: Whether the field value must be unique across all records.

minimum.description: Minimum allowed value for numeric fields.

maximum.description: Maximum allowed value for numeric fields.

enum_values.description: Allowed values when business_type is 'enum'. Empty for non-enum fields.

foreign_key.description: Snake_case name of the target Content when business_type is 'reference'. The runtime creates a foreign key constraint to that Content's 'id' column.

is_auto.description: Whether the field is automatically populated (e.g., 'created_at' timestamps). Auto fields are excluded from create/update forms.

list_type.description: Inner type name when business_type is 'list'. Stored as JSON array in a JSON column.

default_expr.description: Default value expression evaluated at record creation time. If from a CEL expression (DSL: defaults to [expr]), this is the raw expression string (e.g., 'User.Name', '0', 'now'). If from a literal (DSL: defaults to "text"), this is a CEL string literal (e.g., '"normal"'). The runtime evaluates this with the User object and system variables (now, today) in scope. Null means no default — the field is left empty unless provided by the caller.

confidentiality_scopes.description: Confidentiality labels attached to this field. Data in this field is only accessible to identities holding all listed scopes. Empty means unrestricted within the Content's own access grants.

ContentSchema.description: A Content definition — the fundamental data primitive. Each ContentSchema becomes a persistent storage table with an implicit auto-increment 'id' primary key and an optional 'status' column (if has_state_machine is true).

fields.description: Declared fields. The runtime adds implicit 'id' (auto-increment PK) and 'status' (if state machine) columns — these are NOT listed here.

storage_intent.description: Storage hint. 'auto' lets the runtime choose (typically persistent). Future: 'ephemeral' for in-memory-only Content.

has_state_machine.description: Whether this Content has an associated state machine. If true, the runtime adds a 'status' TEXT column and enforces transitions.

initial_state.description: The starting state for new records, set automatically on create. Only meaningful when has_state_machine is true.

confidentiality_scopes.description: Default confidentiality scopes applied to all records in this Content. Individual fields may declare additional scopes. Empty means no Content-level confidentiality restriction.

Verb.description: CRUD operation verb for access control.

AccessGrant.description: A resolved access control rule: 'Anyone with scope X can perform verbs Y on Content Z.'

content.description: Snake_case Content name this grant applies to.

scope.description: The scope string required. Must match a scope declared in auth.scopes.

AuthSpec.description: Authentication configuration. The 'stub' provider is a development-mode cookie-based auth. Production runtimes should implement pluggable providers (SSO, OIDC, etc.).

provider.description: Auth provider identifier. 'stub' = built-in dev auth with role picker.

scopes.description: All declared scopes in the application. Scopes are flat strings — not hierarchical.

roles.description: Named role definitions, each mapping to a set of scopes.

RoleSpec.description: A named role that bundles scopes for assignment to identities.

name.description: Role name from the DSL: 'warehouse manager', 'order clerk'.

TransitionSpec.description: A single allowed state transition, with a required scope.

required_scope.description: Scope the caller's identity must hold to perform this transition.

StateMachineSpec.description: A state machine governing lifecycle transitions. The runtime must enforce that only declared transitions are allowed, and only when the caller holds the required scope.

content_ref.description: Snake_case name of the Content (or Channel/Compute/Boundary) this machine governs.

machine_name.description: Human-readable name for this state machine.

initial_state.description: State assigned to new records on creation.

states.description: All valid states. Multi-word states are lowercase with spaces: 'in progress', 'under review'.

transitions.description: Allowed transitions. Any transition not listed here is forbidden.

primitive_type.description: Which primitive type this state machine is attached to. Usually 'content'.

EventConditionSpec.description: A simple column comparison condition for legacy event triggers.

right_column.description: Snake_case column name to compare against.

EventActionSpec.description: Action to take when an event fires: create a record in target Content with mapped column values.

target_content.description: Snake_case Content name to create a record in.

EventSpec.description: An event rule that fires side-effects when Content changes. Events are the reactive glue between Content types.

source_content.description: Snake_case Content name that triggers this event.

trigger.description: What triggers the event. 'expr' uses the condition_expr expression.

condition.description: Legacy simple condition (column comparison). Null for unconditional or CEL triggers.

action.description: Action to perform when the event fires.

condition_expr.description: CEL expression evaluated against the record. Event fires when this evaluates to truthy.

log_level.description: Severity level for event bus logging.

RouteKind.description: The semantic purpose of a route. Runtimes use this to select the correct handler pattern.

RouteSpec.description: A resolved HTTP API route. The compiler generates routes from Content + AccessGrants. Runtimes may also generate routes dynamically from ContentSchema if this array is empty.

path.description: URL path pattern. Parameters use {name} syntax: '/api/v1/products/{id}'.

content_ref.description: Snake_case Content name this route operates on.

required_scope.description: Scope the caller must hold. Null means no scope check (public route).

lookup_column.description: Column used for {param} in the path. Usually 'id'.

target_state.description: For TRANSITION routes: the target state to transition to.

PropValue.description: A CEL expression prop. When is_expr is true, the runtime must evaluate the value as a CEL expression against the current data context.

ComponentNode.description: A composable UI component in the Presentation tree. Props vary by component type — see the Runtime Implementer's Guide for per-type prop schemas.

type.description: Component type. Determines which props are expected and how the component renders.

props.description: Component-specific properties. Values are either plain strings/numbers/booleans/arrays, or PropValue objects for CEL expressions. See the Implementer's Guide for per-type prop documentation.

style.description: CSS-like visual properties. Reserved for visual editor use. Runtimes may ignore or apply these.

layout.description: Canvas positioning for the visual editor. Runtimes typically ignore this for rendered output.

children.description: Child components. Nesting rules: data_table can contain filter/search/highlight/subscribe/related. form can contain field_input. section can contain anything.

PageEntry.description: A page in the Presentation layer. Each page corresponds to a user story in the DSL and contains a component tree describing the UI.

slug.description: URL-safe page identifier: 'inventory_dashboard'. Used in route paths.

role.description: The role this page belongs to, or 'anonymous' for public pages.

required_scope.description: Scope required to access this page. Null means role membership is sufficient.

NavItemSpec.description: A navigation menu entry. Runtimes render these as the app's main navigation.

label.description: Display text in the navigation menu.

visible_to.description: Role names that can see this nav item. ['all'] means visible to everyone.

badge_content.description: Snake_case Content name. If set, the nav item shows a count badge (SELECT COUNT(*) from that Content).

StreamSpec.description: A Server-Sent Event stream endpoint for real-time push updates.

path.description: URL path for the SSE endpoint: '/api/v1/stream/products'.

ComputeShape.description: The data flow shape of a Compute module. TRANSFORM: 1-to-1. REDUCE: many-to-1. EXPAND: 1-to-many. CORRELATE: many-to-many join. ROUTE: 1-to-conditional-output.

ComputeParamSpec.description: A typed parameter for Compute input or output.

ComputeSpec.description: A Compute module — a pure data transformation with declared inputs, outputs, and a CEL body. Computes are the processing primitive: they read Content, transform it, and write to Content.

input_content.description: Snake_case Content names this Compute reads from.

output_content.description: Snake_case Content names this Compute writes to.

body_lines.description: CEL expression lines forming the Compute body. Evaluated server-side (or client-side if client_safe).

required_scope.description: Scope required to invoke this Compute.

required_role.description: Alternative to required_scope: role name required to invoke.

input_params.description: Typed input parameters beyond Content references.

output_params.description: Typed output parameters beyond Content references.

client_safe.description: Compiler-inferred flag: true when the Compute's CEL body can be safely evaluated on the client (no server-only field access, no side effects). Client runtimes may use this for optimistic local evaluation.

identity_mode.description: Identity propagation mode. 'delegate': the Compute executes with the caller's identity and scopes. 'service': the Compute executes with its own service identity, decoupled from the caller.

required_confidentiality_scopes.description: Confidentiality scopes the caller must hold for all input data consumed by this Compute. The runtime must verify the caller's identity covers these scopes before execution.

output_confidentiality_scope.description: Confidentiality scope stamped on data produced by this Compute. Null means the output inherits scopes from the input data. Non-null enables reclassification — the Compute transforms data from one confidentiality level to another.

field_dependencies.description: Explicit field-level data dependencies. Declares which fields from which Content this Compute reads, along with the confidentiality scopes required to access them.

provider.description: Compute provider. null or 'cel' (default CEL evaluator), 'ai-agent' (autonomous AI), or a CCP package name.

preconditions.description: CEL expressions evaluated before execution. All must return true or execution is rejected (HTTP 412).

postconditions.description: CEL expressions evaluated after execution against the staging snapshot. All must return true or the transaction is rolled back (HTTP 409).

objective.description: Multi-line system prompt for ai-agent provider. Opaque to the runtime — passed to the LLM provider.

strategy.description: Multi-line execution plan for ai-agent provider. Opaque to the runtime — passed to the LLM provider.

trigger.description: Trigger mechanism: 'schedule <interval>' or 'event <name>'. null means API-invoked only.

ChannelDirection.description: Data flow direction. INBOUND: external -> app. OUTBOUND: app -> external. BIDIRECTIONAL: both. INTERNAL: within-boundary only (never crosses a Boundary).

ChannelDelivery.description: Delivery guarantee intent. REALTIME: WebSocket/SSE, may drop. RELIABLE: persistent queue, at-least-once. BATCH: periodic bulk. AUTO: runtime decides based on volume.

ChannelRequirementSpec.description: A scope requirement on a Channel endpoint: who can send or receive on this Channel.

direction.description: Whether this scope is required for sending or receiving.

ChannelSpec.description: A Channel — a declared data flow path. Channels make data movement explicit and enforceable. Every piece of data that crosses a Boundary must go through a Channel.

carries_content.description: Snake_case Content name describing the shape of data this Channel transports.

endpoint.description: Explicit endpoint path (e.g., '/webhooks/orders', '/ws/orders'). Null means no dedicated endpoint (internal routing).

requirements.description: Scope requirements for sending/receiving on this Channel.

BoundaryPropertySpec.description: A computed property exposed by a Boundary. Queryable via Reflection.

expr.description: CEL expression evaluated against the Boundary's contained Content.

BoundarySpec.description: A Boundary — a trust and isolation zone. Boundaries group Content, enforce identity policies, and control data flow via Channels. Data can only cross a Boundary through declared Channels.

boundary_type.description: The kind of Boundary. 'application' is a top-level deployable unit. 'library' provides reusable Content/Compute/Channel definitions. 'module' is a sub-unit within an application. 'configuration' holds settings.

contains_content.description: Snake_case Content names contained in this Boundary.

identity_mode.description: 'inherit': caller identity flows through unchanged. 'restrict': only the listed identity_scopes are allowed within this Boundary.

identity_scopes.description: Scopes allowed within this Boundary when identity_mode is 'restrict'.

properties.description: Computed properties exposed by this Boundary for Reflection queries.

ErrorActionSpec.description: An action taken in response to an error: retry, disable, escalate, create a record, notify, or set a value.

kind.description: Action type. 'retry': re-attempt with optional backoff. 'disable': stop the erroring primitive. 'escalate': promote severity. 'create': create a record. 'notify': send notification. 'set': set a field value.

retry_backoff.description: Whether to use exponential backoff between retries.

target.description: Target Content or Channel for create/notify actions.

expr.description: CEL expression for computed values in set/create actions.

log_level.description: Override log level for this action.

ErrorHandlerSpec.description: An error handling rule that matches errors from a specific source and applies actions.

source.description: Snake_case primitive name this handler matches, or empty string for catch-all.

source_type.description: Which primitive type the source is.

boundary.description: Snake_case Boundary name this handler is scoped to.

condition_expr.description: CEL expression evaluated against the error context. Handler fires only when truthy.

actions.description: Actions to take when this handler matches.

is_catch_all.description: Whether this is a catch-all handler (matches all errors in its scope).

FieldDependency.description: A field-level data dependency for a Compute module. Declares that the Compute reads a specific field from a Content, and the confidentiality scopes required to access it.

content_name.description: Snake_case Content name containing the field.

field_name.description: Snake_case field name within the Content.

confidentiality_scopes.description: Confidentiality scopes required to read this field. Inherited from the field's own confidentiality_scopes if not overridden here.

ReclassificationPoint.description: A declared point where data changes confidentiality level. Reclassification only happens through Compute modules — this makes confidentiality transitions explicit, auditable, and compiler-verified.

compute_name.description: Snake_case name of the Compute module performing the reclassification.

input_scopes.description: Confidentiality scopes of the input data consumed by this Compute.

output_scope.description: Confidentiality scope assigned to the output data produced by this Compute.
